symptoms.symptom_name: Established, satisfied user requesting scheduled injectable contraception

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: routine injectable-contraceptive administration in an eligible user
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuation of an established, tolerated method is the safest and most appropriate contraceptive plan for a satisfied user.

symptoms.symptom_name: Menses began ~36 hours ago (within first 5 days of cycle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active menstrual bleeding within the first 5 days reasonably excludes pregnancy and confers immediate contraceptive cover.

symptoms.symptom_name: No suspicion of pregnancy (active menses, no unprotected gap)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: routine injectable-contraceptive administration in an eligible user
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excluding pregnancy is a mandatory pre-administration safety check; satisfied here by history.

symptoms.symptom_name: No progestogen contraindication on screening (no breast cancer, liver disease or severe depression)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: progestogen contraindication (current breast cancer, active liver disease, severe depression)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Screening negative for the recognised contraindications makes DMPA appropriate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Amenorrhoea with intermittent light spotting on the method

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: routine injectable-contraceptive administration in an eligible user
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: This is the expected, benign bleeding pattern on DMPA — reassuring, not pathological.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: undiagnosed abnormal uterine bleeding needing work-up
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pattern fits known method-related change rather than new intermenstrual or post-coital bleeding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured baseline vital signs (BP, HR, temperature)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: routine injectable-contraceptive administration in an eligible user
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal baseline vitals were documented in person; the AI Doctor cannot measure these and does not require them to confirm eligibility, though a patient-offered home BP could be considered.

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative — not performed today because active menses within the first 5 days already excludes pregnancy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: None here; only required if cycle timing were uncertain.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A POC pregnancy test is the fallback exclusion when menstrual timing is uncertain; not needed here and not obtainable over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required — active menstrual bleeding within the first 5 days already excludes pregnancy. Ordering it here is unnecessary over-investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline blood pressure check at the in-person administration visit
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A one-off baseline BP is reasonable good practice at administration, but it is a routine in-person step, not a telehealth prerequisite.
